title: Sterculia gengmaensis
taxonomy: Sterculia gengmaensis is classified within the family Malvaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. The genus Sterculia is a group of trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. Sterculia gengmaensis is specifically classified as a species within the genus Sterculia. It is native to Yunnan Province in southwestern China. The classification of Sterculia gengmaensis is further supported by its unique morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.
uses: Sterculia gengmaensis has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine, where its bark is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. It is also used to reduce inflammation and pain. The plant is valued for its antiseptic and antipyretic properties, which help in treating various health conditions. Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Additionally, it is used in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported benefits in treating various diseases.

The plant's roots and leaves are rich in bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities.
